claims.description: Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass (932333893, already on origin/main): the Navigation shell matrix's 'What's actually shipped today' build-profile table and its V2 column against apps/mobile/eas.json still hold — untouched by this rework, re-read against current source and confirmed accurate.

Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass and re-checked against current source this session: navigation-contract.ts is still 643 lines; STUDY_TABS still opens at line 153; V2_ROUTES is still at line 178; resolveCanEnter still opens at line 457. All four line numbers read directly against the file at this branch, not assumed from the prior pass.

Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass: the V2-CHROME-01/MENTOR-01/MENTOR-02/SUBJECTS-01/JOURNAL-01/SCOPE-01/SCOPE-02 rows' substantive Flow/Audience/Gating content (person-scope chips, person selection, scope-preserving tab changes, landing routes, back/exit, authorization boundaries) is untouched by this rework — only their Coverage cells changed, per AC-5.

Bounce-3 finding: the Status legend's own tagging test ("Which rows get one of these three tags") exempted a row from tagging whenever its content was judged shell-independent, so 233 of 288 retained rows — including ACCOUNT-07 through ACCOUNT-52 — carried no legacy-* tag at all, contradicting the AC's literal "every retained V0/V1 row is tagged" requirement. Fix: the exemption sentence and its section-scoped "applied exhaustively under this test" enumeration were removed and replaced with a universal rule — every retained row gets exactly one tag; shell-differential rows get the matching current/superseded/historical tag, and genuinely shell-independent rows still get legacy-current plus a short accurate note explaining why (renders pre-shell, reached via equivalent surfaces regardless of shell, orphaned/dormant in every flag state, etc.) rather than being exempted. All 227 previously-untagged rows across every section — Auth and Access, Profiles/Family/Consent/Account, Home/Navigation/Subject Setup, Learning/Chat/Library/Retention/Progress, Practice Hub (incl. Quiz/Dictation), Homework and Parent Experience, Billing and Monetization, Regression and System Flows (QA-*), and Cross-Cutting Behaviors (CC-*) — are now tagged; the only rows left untagged are the 6 new V2-only rows in "V2 Shell — Mentor, Subjects, Journal, Account" (V2-CHROME-01, V2-MENTOR-01/02, V2-SUBJECTS-01, V2-JOURNAL-01, V2-SCOPE-01), which have no V0/V1 predecessor to retain. Three cross-cutting rows (CC-19, CC-20, CC-22) were identified as genuinely shell-*specific* rather than shell-independent during the tagging pass and tagged legacy-current with a note naming the specific V0/V1-only mechanism (route-access gate machinery, Recaps/family-bridge return targets, the V0-only app-help map) instead of the generic shell-independent note. All 288 row IDs now carry exactly one legacy-* tag (verified: `grep -c` for the three tag strings against the full row-ID list yields 288, matching `extractDefinedRowIds`' count minus the 6 V2-only rows plus their own non-applicability). Scope note for the reviewer: whether the two audience-agnostic sections (QA-*, CC-*) should carry this V0/V1-legacy tag at all was a judgment call, not explicit in the reviewer's cited examples (which were all ACCOUNT-*) — resolved in favor of the non-narrowing reading of the AC's literal "every retained V0/V1 row" and the ruling's "every other pre-V2 row", since excluding them would itself be an AC-narrowing decision reserved for PM ruling, not this rework. SCOPED-FINAL-FIX (bounce-3, PM-authorized 2026-07-20): added a fourth Status-legend tag v2-current and applied it to all 7 V2-shell rows (V2-CHROME-01, V2-MENTOR-01/02, V2-SUBJECTS-01, V2-JOURNAL-01, V2-SCOPE-01, V2-SCOPE-02), removing the incorrect legacy-superseded from V2-SCOPE-02 — resolving the reviewer bounce-3 V2-tag consistency finding; the legend now defines four tags (three legacy-insurance + v2-current for the target shell).

A new 'Manifest Reconciliation (AC-5)' section reconciles all 187 distinct manifest files cited across the doc's 449 backtick citations, per the cite-rot guard's own count (not a subset of batch-touched rows), with a per-manifest path-exists / runs-under-CI-trigger / proves-flow table. The CI-trigger facts are mechanical: each Maestro flow's real tags: frontmatter is read against ci-maestro-plan.mjs's suite-selection logic (nightly cron vs PR gate vs the v2 workflow_dispatch suite vs untriggered tags like weekly/blocked/manual/devclient), and each Playwright spec against its playwright.config.ts project testMatch (web smoke vs full suite vs opt-in-only projects). 'Proves flow' starts from each citing row's own existing coverage annotation and is downgraded where this pass found the mechanical fact narrows or contradicts it. Two concrete drift findings corrected: (1) the V2 Shell section's intro falsely claimed no dedicated Maestro/Playwright flow targets the 3-tab shell and mis-cited the scope-switching journey as a nonexistent 'V2-05' — three real, already-landed native Maestro flows (v2-shell-navigation.yaml, v2-supporter-scope-journey.yaml, v2-subject-create-round-trip.yaml, wired to the v2 workflow_dispatch suite) are now cited against the correct V2-CHROME-01/MENTOR-01/MENTOR-02/SUBJECTS-01/JOURNAL-01/SCOPE-01 rows; (2) BILLING-06's 'PARKED / out-of-manifest' claim for billing/child-paywall.yaml is stale — the file's live YAML tags: frontmatter carries 'nightly' (so the tag-based nightly-suite selection in ci-maestro-plan.mjs does select it, contradicting the file's own header comment), and the same file's own 'KNOWN BREAK' comment documents that it currently times out post-ACCOUNT-04 rather than passing; both are marked in the row and the table, not silently fixed (test-flow/CI-config changes are out of this docs-only item's scope, flagged as a Gap instead).
Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass: the 'What's actually shipped today' table and its no-universal-default wording are untouched by this rework.

Bounce-3 finding: the prior pass's checkFlagTokens only verified flag-name spelling against feature-flags.ts, never whether a row's declared shell/flag state actually matches what the navigation contract resolves — a row could name a real flag while asserting the wrong tabs and still pass. Fix (design pre-approved by the shepherd before implementation): a fourth mechanical check, checkNavShellMatrixTabShapes, bounded to the doc's 'Navigation shell matrix' table — the one place that makes machine-checkable tab-shape claims via a cited constant name (LEARNER_TABS, GUARDIAN_TABS, PARENT_PROXY_TABS, FAMILY_MODE_TABS, STUDY_MODE_TABS, STUDY_TABS, FAMILY_TABS, PROXY_TABS, V2_TABS) — not the 288 flow rows' free prose (that would be a full semantic-diff harness, explicitly out of scope). For every matrix cell that both cites one of these constants AND spells out the tab names it claims (cells using 'same'/count-only shorthand make no independently-checkable claim and are skipped, not failed), the check locates the citation's own enclosing parenthetical group via a balanced top-level-paren scan (so trailing commentary after the citation, e.g. '— More tab removed entirely', is never read into the claim, and a nested citation like `(app)/_layout.tsx:827` inside one group doesn't fracture group boundaries), extracts the claimed tab names from the span between the previous group and this one, and compares them (as sets, order-independent) against the constant's real `new Set([...])` literal, regex-extracted directly from source (no import/execution of the RN modules). Hardening H1: a cited constant whose literal can't be resolved (non-literal declaration — spread, function call, conditional, renamed export) is reported as a failure, never silently skipped or passed — verified all 9 constants are resolvable literals; a synthetic non-literal case is covered by a dedicated test. Hardening H2: ran the new check against the current doc — all 6 currently machine-checkable claims pass (LEARNER_TABS, V2_TABS, GUARDIAN_TABS, FAMILY_MODE_TABS, FAMILY_TABS, PARENT_PROXY_TABS each match their real Set exactly; STUDY_MODE_TABS/STUDY_TABS/PROXY_TABS are cited in the matrix but never spelled out with explicit tab names in any cell, so there is currently nothing false to catch for them — not a loophole, a pre-existing doc-authoring choice using shorthand). Co-located test suite adds 11 cases: literal extraction (pass/absent/non-literal-H1), and the check's pass/fail/skip/H1/order-independence/trailing-comment-exclusion/clause-scoping paths. Wired into the existing main()/docs-checks.yml job — no new script, no new CI job.

Refinement notes still satisfied: no product code changed this rework either (docs/flows/mobile-app-flow-inventory.md plus the one existing tooling script, extended). e2e-web smoke is still never treated as full-layout-claim evidence; the new AC-5 table makes the smoke/full/opt-in distinction explicit per manifest rather than leaving it implicit. AC-7's guard additions stayed mechanical (regex/set-membership against real source), not a new test harness.
